cot_content:
Okay, let's think about this question. It's about how to distinguish between the right and left earbuds of a pair of earphones without looking.

The options are:
A. Tie a small knot at the connecting end of the left hand one.
B. Tie a small knot at the speaker end of the left hand one.

I need to determine which one is the correct method to tell them apart.

Earbuds typically have a plastic or rubber tip that goes into the ear (the speaker end), and a wire that connects to the audio source (the connecting end). When you wear them, the speaker end is the part that goes in your ear, and the connecting end is the part that connects to the wire.

To tell them apart without looking, you need a marker that is on the part that is not in your ear when you're using them. If you tie a knot at the connecting end (the part that's connected to the wire), you can feel that knot when you take the earbuds out, helping you determine which is the left one. If you tie a knot at the speaker end (the part that goes in your ear), you'd have to put it in your ear to feel it, which defeats the purpose of being able to tell them apart without looking.

So, tying a knot at the connecting end (option A) would be the method to distinguish between the right and left earbuds without looking, since you can feel the knot on the left earbud's wire when you remove them.

Therefore, the correct answer is A.
label: minority
